title: Frame Budgets, Profiling, Allocations, Collision Cost, and Responsiveness · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for frame budgets, profiling, allocations, collision cost, and responsiveness and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, pygame-ce, SDL, display, input, audio, filesystem, packaging, GPU, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for representative frame-time and capacity evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case…
objectives: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.
Profile warm representative waves, worst supported entity counts, stalls, resize, pause, and low-power targets before optimizing.
Measure per-frame allocations and cache immutable assets, transforms, masks, text, or pools only when evidence justifies complexity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Reconstruct the defensible process · Quiz Frame Budget
steps.instruction:

content.text: A quiz Asteroids team handles case 4582 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Allocate a target frame budget across event, simulation, collision, rendering, presentation, and audio work using percentiles.

Write the quiz evidence note for Performance Regression: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Asteroids team handles case 4532 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to profile update and render phases against percentile frame-time, allocation, entity, collision, and responsiveness budgets without changing behavior; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate percentile frame time, input-to-feedback latency, memory, load time, and semantic replay equivalence by target.
